column5: Display Desc

A <rest> is counted when a unit uses a Move in which they stand still, or when a unit ends their turn while still having AP. Some statuses and tile effects only trigger on each <rest>, like [poison] tiles.

<Travel> is any linear, ground-based movement from one adjacent tile to another. Standard 'walking' movement is <travel>, but jumping/teleporting are not. <Travel> incurs things like [ice] tile slipperiness, while teleporting on to an ice tile would not.

Some minor actions/penalties cause Action Point crystals to receive a <fracture line>, but not immediately break (ie. AP is not expended). AP crystals can be <fractured> twice safely, but on their 3rd <fracture>, break and are expended. This makes some minor actions, such as standard unit [travel], 'free' or very low-cost. [Elec] damage always causes 1 <AP fracture>.
<Line-of-sight> (or 'LOS') checks whether one unit can directly see another without other units or obstacles in its way. Some moves require <line-of-sight> to be usable.
<Shields> lessen incoming damage by the total amount of <shields> a unit has, unless the incoming attack is [piercing] or [bypassing].
Damage which will bypass 1 layer of [shield].
Damage which will bypass *all* layers of [shield].
A type of [travel] movement externally caused by another unit. Does not cause direct damage (unlike [knockback]), although damage is still possible if the unit is <pushed> into a hazardous position.

A type of [travel] movement which unlike a [push], causes damage when it *cannot* be fully completed. If a unit would be shoved 2 tiles away but a rock is in the way, the 2 tiles of motion are received as 2 damage instead. Not all externally-caused movement is <knockback>; usually <knockback> is only applied if the movement is violent or abrupt.

<Reactions> are actions a unit takes automatically when certain conditions are met, whether it's that unit's turn or not. Every <reaction> is different and has its own trigger for what causes it, such as the Knight's 'Counter Stance' move <reacting> to incoming melee damage.

Units that are <airborne> (or 'hovering') in the air are not at all affected by tile types, nor take [ground] attack damage/effects. While <airborne>, units are treated as [lightweight] regardless of their actual weight class.
Units that are too <light> to be affected by certain ground effects, like [ice] tiles, but are easily moved by winds, like [air tunnels].
Units that cannot be easily moved by others, and typically ignore [push] or [knockback] effects. Notably, a <heavy> unit on an [ice] tile behaves like a standard-weight unit.
<Woodland> units bypass the entry penalty of [overgrowth] tiles, but still receive the benefits.

Units of immense physical force; their attacks are always [bludgeoning]. <Smashers> also treat [jagged] tiles as standard, neither being affected by nor resetting it - convenient, given they often create them.
<Sprout> damage always turns any impacted tiles into [overgrowth].
<Water> attacks will always break 1/4 of a [shield] on impact, even when dealing 0 damage. Cools & erases [ember] tiles.
<Ice> attacks always cause the [Frigid] status to impacted units. If an ice attack hits an [ember] tile, it will convert the tile into a [puddle].

<Fire> damage is always [piercing], and stacks with other [piercing] sources. <Fire> also does 1 extra damage to units on [overgrowth] tiles and burns it away, or converts [ice] tiles to [puddles]. Units already on a [puddle] tile will receive -1 damage, and the [puddle] will evaporate.
<Poison> attacks always cause the [Poisoned] status to impacted units, even if dealing 0 damage.
<Ground> attacks do not affect [airborne] units; they commonly also ignore cover and [line-of-sight].
Always turns the tile below impacted units to [static], immediately affecting them. If the unit was on a [puddle] tile, does +1 damage to them first.
<Bludgeoning> damage always expends 1 AP of the impacted unit.
<Bleed> damage turns the tile below the impacted unit to [blood].
<Enchanted> tiles can never be altered into a different tile type.
<Jagged> tiles deal 1 damage and penalize 1 AP upon a unit entering them, after which they revert to a standard tile. [Smasher] units treat these like regular tiles.
<Overgrowth> tiles cost 1 AP to enter (unless the unit is [woodland]), but lessens incoming damage by 1 while within it. Receives +1 extra damage and is destroyed by [fire] attacks.
Ending your turn on an <ember> tile incurs 1 [fire] damage, but also confers +1 AP towards your next turn.
Both entering and [resting] on a poison tile causes 1/4 [shield-bypassing] damage.
Slippery! Any unit that ends an action/movement on an <ice> tile via [travel] will slide to the far side of it, if possible. [Lightweight] units are too light to be affected.
<Puddle> tiles will break 1/4 [shield] each time a unit [rests] in it. Units in puddles also receive +1 damage from [elec] attacks, and -1 from [fire] attacks, both of which 'expend' the <puddle>.
<Static> tiles lock the joints of units on them. Units must [rest] to clear the static, and cannot leave the tile or use non-[rest] actions until they have done so.

On its own, a <blood> tile has no effect; however, certain powerful moves require the presence of <blood> tiles to be usable. Typically, any unit capable of dealing [bleed] damage has moves taking advantage of <blood> tiles.

Tiles which attempt to [push] units in a fixed direction. [Lightweight] and [airborne] units are [pushed] immediately; standard-weight units are [pushed] upon [resting], and [heavy] units are unafffected.

When a unit is [rests] next to an unoccupied <lodestone> tile, it is pulled onto it. This effect is resisted if the unit is next to multiple <lodestones> at once, or already on a <lodestone>. <Lodestones> will not pull [heavy] units unless they are on an [ice] tile.
